questions.question: What is the safe cold email limit per inbox per day for Google Workspace?
questions.explanation: While Google's technical limit is 500 emails/day, the safe cold email limit is only 30-50/day. The official limit was designed for transactional email and internal communication. Cold email at 200/day from one inbox triggers spam heuristics: sudden volume spikes, low engagement rates, templated content, and concentrated sending patterns.

questions.question: What happened when David sent 200 cold emails from a single inbox in one day?

options.text: Inbox placement dropped from 92% to 23% within 48 hours, requiring 60 days of domain rest and $12,000+ in lost pipeline
Nothing — he was within the official limit

questions.explanation: Despite being under the 500/day official limit, David's 200 cold emails triggered every spam heuristic: sudden volume spike (35 to 200), low engagement rate, template similarity, and concentrated sending. He had to rest the domain 60 days, buy 4 new domains, and lost ~$12,000 in pipeline value.

questions.question: Why do official email limits and safe cold email limits differ so dramatically?

options.text: Cold email has low engagement rates, unknown contacts, templated content, and batch sending — all patterns that trigger spam filters differently than normal email
Safe limits are made up by email marketing companies

questions.explanation: Normal email has 20-80% reply rates, known contacts, varied content, and natural timing. Cold email has 1-5% reply rates, unknown contacts, templated content, and batch sending. These behavioral differences mean filters treat cold email much more suspiciously, requiring significantly lower volume to maintain reputation.

questions.question: Inbox rotation — spreading sends across multiple inboxes and domains — is essential for safely scaling cold email volume beyond 50/day.
questions.explanation: True. To safely send 200-400 cold emails per day, you need 4-12 inboxes across 3-4 domains, each sending only 30-50 emails. Inbox rotation tools (Instantly, Smartlead) automatically distribute sends across inboxes, maintaining safe per-inbox volume while achieving scale.

questions.question: Design your inbox rotation strategy. How many domains and inboxes do you need to reach your target daily send volume while staying within safe limits? Show the math: target volume / safe per-inbox limit = inboxes needed.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response includes a specific target daily volume, correctly calculates inboxes needed (volume / 30-50 per inbox), plans for 2-3 inboxes per domain, and considers domain diversity. Look for understanding that the math must account for warm-up capacity and growth headroom.
